description: Spain builds a stone fort in New Mexico, creating a lasting military and government headquarters for the northern frontier.
Father Kino establishes a desert outpost, starting a massive network of missions that eventually reached the Pacific coast.
Spain builds its first fort and mission in California, securing its first permanent foothold on the Alta California coast.
The Continental Congress signs the Declaration of Independence, officially breaking free from British rule.
The United States buys a massive piece of land from France, doubling its size and moving its border next to Spanish territory.
Mexico wins its war of independence against Spain, taking over all the wild and vast northern frontier lands.
Settlers and locals revolt against Mexico to declare and win the independence of the short-lived Republic of Texas.
The peace treaty ending the Mexican-American War cedes California and the Southwest to the United States.
